title: gomphandra laxiflora
intro: Gomphandra laxiflora, also known as the Malaysian tree huckleberry or wild blueberry tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stemonuraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asia for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gomphandra laxiflora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Gomphandra laxiflora can be prepared as a decoction or infused tea, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
